---
title: "Project 1 Checkpoint 1"
author: "Amanda Dong"
format: html
---

## Measles Dataset

We are provided with data from the World Health Organization on measles and 
rubella cases across the world from the years 2018 through 2026. The total amount
of measles and rubella cases have been recorded. This includes suspected cases
and lab confirmed cases. We are given the data in two time frames: by month and
by year.

## How was the dataset cleaned?

The names of the variables were cleaned using the janitor package.

## Possible Research Questions
**With the given Data:**

Two possible research questions we can investigate using the given data:

  1. What regions/countries tend to have more measles and rubella cases?
  
  2. Do the number of cases change depending on the time of year?
  
**With Supplemental Data:**

  1. How does access to healthcare affect the number of measles and rubella cases?
  
  2. Do higher populated areas tend to have more cases of measles and rubella?
  
## Data Visualization Sketches
What regions tend to have more measles and rubella cases?
```{r, echo = FALSE}
knitr::include_graphics('/Users/amandadong/Documents/stat 431/project1/graph1.png')
```

Do higher populated areas tend to have more cases of measles and rubella?
```{r, echo = FALSE}
knitr::include_graphics('/Users/amandadong/Documents/stat 431/project1/graph2.png')
```
